description:
You are an ethnoastronomer developing content for a museum planetarium exhibit called 'One Sky, Many Stories.' You need to generate reference images showing how three different civilizations — Western European, Ancient Egyptian, and Chinese — interpreted the same region of the night sky around the prominent star Betelgeuse.

Configure the observation site to the Great Pyramid of Giza, Egypt (29.9792°N, 31.1342°E, altitude 60 meters) for December 21, 2023, at 22:00 UTC. For exhibit-quality views, disable the atmosphere and ground/landscape, and enable both constellation lines and labels. 

Capture screenshots centered on Betelgeuse for each of the three sky cultures: Western, Egyptian, and Chinese. After capturing the images, ensure the sky culture is set to Egyptian. Screenshots should be saved to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/. Finally, write exhibit reference notes to /home/ga/Desktop/exhibit_sky_notes.txt including the observation site (Giza), the date (December 21, 2023), the three sky cultures compared, and a note identifying Betelgeuse as the reference star.
